questions.question: Explain the term 'controlled access' in the context of computer networks. Provide an example of how it can be implemented using a protocol.
questions.hint: Describe the advantages of using controlled access in wireless networks.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a carrier sense multiple access with collision detection (CSMA/CD) protocol in a token ring network? Explain its limitations in terms of network efficiency.
questions.hint: Compare the performance of CSMA/CD with other protocols like CSMA/CA.

questions.question: A wireless local area network (WLAN) uses a distributed token ring protocol with a transmission rate of 11 Mbps. If the token is dropped during transmission, the network will suffer from collisions. What type of error correction technique can be used to mitigate this issue?
questions.hint: Explain the advantages of using forward error correction over other error correction techniques.
